title: Check Point CloudGuard Network Security Events
dataSources: `sourcetype=cp_log` (CloudGuard logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud)
spl:
index=cloud sourcetype="cp_log" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(product),"(?i)cloudguard|cloud.?network") OR match(lower(origin_sic_name),"(?i)aws|azure|gcp")
| stats count by product, action, protection_name, cloud_provider
| sort -count
description: CloudGuard Network extends Check Point Quantum prevention to cloud workloads in AWS, Azure, and GCP. Security events from cloud-deployed gateways provide the same IPS, threat prevention, and access control visibility as on-prem — critical for hybrid environments.
value: CloudGuard Network extends Check Point Quantum prevention to cloud workloads in AWS, Azure, and GCP. Security events from cloud-deployed gateways provide the same IPS, threat prevention, and access control visibility as on-prem — critical for hybrid environments.
implementation: Deploy CloudGuard Network in AWS VPC, Azure VNET, or GCP VPC and forward logs to Smart-1 Cloud or on-prem Log Server. Normalize `cloud_provider` from SIC name or tags. Alert on critical threats in cloud environments. Report on cloud vs on-prem detection ratios for security posture.
visualization: Bar chart (events by cloud provider), Table (top protections), Pie chart (action distribution), Line chart (event trend).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.action | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Check Point blocks from known pen tests, SandBlast detonations, or temporary policy or identity exceptions; confirm in SmartConsole before treating as a new campaign.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cp_log` (CloudGuard logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy CloudGuard Network in AWS VPC, Azure VNET, or GCP VPC and forward logs to Smart-1 Cloud or on-prem Log Server. Normalize `cloud_provider` from SIC name or tags. Alert on critical threats in cloud environments. Report on cloud vs on-prem detection ratios for security posture.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype="cp_log" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(product),"(?i)cloudguard|cloud.?network") OR match(lower(origin_sic_name),"(?i)aws|azure|gcp")
| stats count by product, action, protection_name, cloud_provider
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Check Point CloudGuard Network Security Events** — CloudGuard Network extends Check Point Quantum prevention to cloud workloads in AWS, Azure, and GCP. Security events from cloud-deployed gateways provide the same IPS, threat prevention, and access control visibility as on-prem — critical for hybrid environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp_log` (CloudGuard logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud; **sourcetype**: cp_log. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud, sourcetype="cp_log", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(product),"(?i)cloudguard|cloud.?network") OR match(lower(origin_sic_name),"(?i)aws|azure|gcp")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by product, action, protection_name, cloud_provider** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.action | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Check Point CloudGuard Network Security Events** — CloudGuard Network extends Check Point Quantum prevention to cloud workloads in AWS, Azure, and GCP. Security events from cloud-deployed gateways provide the same IPS, threat prevention, and access control visibility as on-prem — critical for hybrid environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp_log` (CloudGuard logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (events by cloud provider), Table (top protections), Pie chart (action distribution), Line chart (event trend).
grandmaExplanation: We use Check Point data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Check Point CloudGuard Network Security Events". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
